# Framework & Bundler Ecosystem -- March 2026

_Scan date: 2026-03-27_
_Period: January 26 -- March 27, 2026_
_Previous report: 2026-03 (dated 2026-03-24, 2026-03-23, 2026-03-20, 2026-03-18, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-05), 2026-02_

## Executive Summary

Key developments since the last scan (Mar 24):

- **Angular 22.0.0-next.5** (Mar 25) -- **OnPush as default change detection**, Hammer.js removed, duplicate selector compile-time errors. Major breaking changes for v22.
- **Angular 21.2.6** (Mar 25) -- patch: image fetch fix, CSS comment fix, animation regression fix, inject migration fix for multi-project workspaces.
- **Vite 8.0.3** (Mar 26) -- third stable patch. Upgrades Rolldown to RC.12. Fixes CSS style caching across entries, non-ASCII sourcemap handling, SSR meta rewrite bug.
- **Rspack 2.0.0-beta.9** (Mar 27) -- Notable: `create-rspack` updated to TypeScript 6, new `detectSyntax` option for swc-loader, RSC tree-shaking fixes.
- **Vue 3.5.31** (Mar 25) + **3.6.0-beta.9** (Mar 26) -- stable patch and beta progression.
- **Rolldown 1.0.0-rc.12** (Mar 25) -- fixes non-deterministic chunk generation, lazy-barrel loading improvements. Powers Vite 8.0.3.
- **Remix 3.0.0-alpha.4** (Mar 25) -- adds auth middleware, CORS middleware, CSRF middleware, COP (cross-origin protection) middleware. Breaking: `data-table` API restructured (`QueryBuilder` removed, replaced by `Query`/`db.exec()`).
- TypeScript 6.0 STABLE (6.0.2), Next.js 16.2.1, SWC 1.15.21, esbuild 0.27.4, webpack 5.105.4 unchanged from previous scan.

The overall picture remains: **TypeScript 6.0 stable is the #1 priority**. The Angular 22-next.5 release amplifies urgency with **OnPush as the new default change detection** -- this will be a significant migration for existing Angular projects. Rspack 2.0 is already adopting TS 6 in its create-rspack tooling, confirming ecosystem-wide TS 6 momentum.

## Compatibility Risks

| #   | Risk                                                                                                        | Severity       | Nx Plugin(s) Affected                  |
| --- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| 1   | **TypeScript 6.0 STABLE** -- deprecated defaults Nx relies on (see detailed section below)                  | **CRITICAL**   | **All generators that emit tsconfig**  |
| 2   | **Vite 8 STABLE** -- `rolldownOptions` replaces `rollupOptions`, esbuild deprecated, Lightning CSS default  | **URGENT**     | `@nx/vite`, all Vite-based plugins     |
| 3   | Rspack 2.0 drops Node 18, pure ESM, renames `webpackChunk*` to `rspackChunk*`                               | HIGH           | `@nx/rspack`, `@nx/module-federation`  |
| 4   | Next.js 16 `middleware.ts` -> `proxy.ts` rename; `next lint` removed                                       | HIGH           | `@nx/next`                             |
| 5   | Nuxt 4.4 vue-router v5 upgrade                                                                              | MEDIUM         | `@nx/nuxt`                             |
| 6   | Angular 22.0.0-next.5: **OnPush default**, Hammer.js removed, duplicate selector errors, selectorless components | **HIGH**       | `@nx/angular`                          |

---

## Angular

**Latest stable:** 21.2.6 (Mar 25) | **Pre-release:** 22.0.0-next.5 (Mar 25)

### Releases in window

| Version       | Date    | Type             |
| ------------- | ------- | ---------------- |
| 22.0.0-next.5 | Mar 25  | Pre-release      |
| 22.0.0-next.4 | Mar 19  | Pre-release      |
| 22.0.0-next.3 | Mar 12  | Pre-release      |
| 22.0.0-next.2 | Mar 11  | Pre-release      |
| 22.0.0-next.1 | Mar 5   | Pre-release      |
| 22.0.0-next.0 | Mar 4   | Pre-release      |
| 21.2.6        | Mar 25  | Stable           |
| 21.2.5        | Mar 18  | Stable           |
| 21.2.4        | Mar 12  | Stable           |
| 21.2.3        | Mar 11  | Stable           |
| 21.2.2        | Mar 9   | Stable           |
| 21.2.1        | Mar 4   | Stable           |
| 21.2.0        | Feb 25  | Stable           |
| 21.1.0-21.1.6 | Jan-Feb | Stable (patches) |

### Key changes in 21.2

- **SignalFormControl** (`@angular/forms/signals/compat`) -- bridge between Signal Forms and classic Reactive Forms, pluggable into FormGroup/FormArray
- **FormRoot directive** -- auto-adds `novalidate`, prevents native browser validation interference
- **Arrow functions in template expressions** -- `(click)="() => doStuff()"`
- **`ChangeDetectionStrategy.Eager`** alias for Default
- **TypeScript 6 compatibility**
- **Nested animations** support
- **`instanceof` operator** in template compiler
- Null handling improvements for number inputs in Signal Forms

### Angular 22 direction (next.0 through next.5)

- **`ChangeDetectionStrategy` default is now `OnPush`** (next.5) -- **MAJOR BREAKING CHANGE**. Components without explicit `changeDetection` will be OnPush. Use `ChangeDetectionStrategy.Eager` to keep previous behavior.
- **Hammer.js integration removed** (next.5) -- `platform-browser` no longer ships HammerJS support. Apps must provide their own implementation.
- **Duplicate selector compile-time error (NG8023)** (next.5) -- elements matching multiple selectors now throw at compile time.
- **HTML comment support in templates** (next.5) -- `<!-- ... -->` comments preserved in compiled output.
- **Signal Forms enhancements** (next.5) -- `reloadValidation` for manual async validation trigger, `debounce` option for `validateAsync`/`validateHttp`, `FieldState.getError()`, `number|null` binding to text inputs.
- **Selectorless components** -- import components directly by class, no selectors needed in templates
- **`ngNoCva`** -- opt-out mechanism for ControlValueAccessors (next.4)
- **Template & reactive support for FormValueControl (FVC)** -- deepening signal-first forms (next.4)
- **`TestBed.getFixture` -> `TestBed.getLastFixture`** rename (next.4)
- **`checkNoChanges` removed from public API** (next.4) -- breaking change for v22
- Signal-based forms maturation (signal-first APIs deepening)
- Consolidation of post-Zone.js era
- Expected stable: ~May 2026

### Nx impact

- 21.2.6 is a patch, no breaking changes. Nx 22.3+ already supports Angular 21.
- **OnPush default in v22 is the biggest Angular migration event** -- every existing component without explicit `changeDetection` will need either `Eager` annotation or verification that it works with OnPush. Plan migration generator.
- **Hammer.js removal** -- Nx generators should stop including HammerJS setup for Angular 22+ projects.
- **Duplicate selector NG8023** -- existing Nx workspace code that accidentally duplicates selectors will now fail to compile on v22.
- SignalFormControl is experimental; no generator changes needed yet.
- TS 6 compat means Angular projects can adopt TS 6 stable.
- Monitor v22-next for selectorless component support in generators -- this will require template/import generation changes.
- `checkNoChanges` removal and `TestBed.getLastFixture` rename are breaking for v22 tests -- plan migration generator.

---

## React

**Latest stable:** 19.2.4 (Jan 26)

### Releases in window

- v19.2.4, v19.1.5, v19.0.4 (all Jan 26) -- security patches across all v19 lines

### Context (React 19.2 features, shipped Oct 2025)

- `<Activity />` component (hidden/visible modes for pre-rendering)
- `useEffectEvent()` for non-reactive logic extraction
- View Transitions support (`useId` prefix changed from `:r:` to `_r_`)
- SSR streaming batching improvements

### Nx impact

- No breaking changes. Patch releases only.
- `useId` prefix change could affect snapshot tests.
- View Transitions and `<Activity />` are opt-in; no generator changes needed.

## Next.js

**Latest stable:** 16.2.1 (Mar 20) | **Canary:** 16.2.1-canary.10 (Mar 26)
**Backport:** 15.5.14 (Mar 19)

### Releases in window

| Version                    | Date      | Type                    |
| -------------------------- | --------- | ----------------------- |
| **16.2.1**                 | Mar 20    | **STABLE**              |
| **16.2.0**                 | Mar 18    | **STABLE**              |
| 16.2.1-canary.0-10         | Mar 18-26 | Canary                  |
| 16.2.0-canary.29-104       | Feb-Mar   | Canary (104 releases)   |
| 16.1.3-16.1.7              | Jan-Mar   | Stable patches          |
| 15.5.10-15.5.14            | Jan-Mar   | Legacy/backport         |
| Security wave (15.0-16.0)  | Jan 26    | CVE-2025-66478 patches  |

### Next.js 16 breaking changes (recap -- these are already stable)

**Removals:**

- Sync `params`/`searchParams`/`cookies()`/`headers()` access -- must use `await`
- `next lint` command removed -- use ESLint/Biome directly
- `experimental.ppr` and `experimental.dynamicIO` flags -- replaced by `cacheComponents: true`
- AMP support entirely removed
- `serverRuntimeConfig`/`publicRuntimeConfig` -- use env vars

**Behavior changes:**

- **Turbopack is default bundler** -- webpack via `--webpack` flag
- **`proxy.ts` replaces `middleware.ts`** -- Edge runtime not supported in proxy, Node.js runtime only
- **Parallel routes require explicit `default.js` files**
- **Node.js 20.9+ minimum** (Node 18 dropped)
- `revalidateTag()` requires cacheLife profile as 2nd argument

**New APIs:**

- Cache Components (`"use cache"` directive)
- `updateTag()` -- Server Actions API with read-your-writes semantics
- `refresh()` -- Server Actions API for uncached data refresh
- React Compiler support stable (`reactCompiler: true`, not default)

### Next.js 16.1 additions

- Turbopack File System Caching stable for dev
- Experimental Bundle Analyzer for Turbopack
- `next dev --inspect` debugging support

### Next.js 16.2 additions (Mar 18)

**Performance:**

- **~87% faster `next dev` startup** (Time-to-URL)
- **25-60% faster SSR rendering** -- React contribution (`JSON.parse` reviver optimization, eliminates C++/JS boundary crossing)
- **2-20x faster `ImageResponse`** -- basic to complex images
- Default font changed from Noto Sans to Geist Sans in `ImageResponse`

**Stable features:**

- **Adapters API stable** -- platforms can customize build process (`adapterPath` config)
- **`transitionTypes` prop on `<Link>`** -- array of View Transition types for navigation animations (App Router only)
- **Server Function logging** in dev terminal (name, args, execution time, file)
- **Hydration diff indicator** -- clear server/client diff in error overlay (`+ Client` / `- Server`)
- **`next start --inspect`** -- attach Node.js debugger to production server
- **Error causes in dev overlay** -- `Error.cause` chain display up to 5 levels
- **Multiple icon formats** -- `icon.png` + `icon.svg` auto-handled for browser fallback
- **Redesigned default error page** (500 page)

**Experimental features:**

- `unstable_catchError()` -- granular error boundaries at component level
- `unstable_retry()` in `error.tsx` -- built-in retry with `router.refresh()` + `reset()`
- `experimental.prefetchInlining` -- bundles all segment data into single response
- `experimental.cachedNavigations` -- caches static/dynamic RSC data from navigations
- `experimental.appNewScrollHandler` -- reworked scroll/focus management

**AI-focused:**

- `AGENTS.md` included in `create-next-app` templates
- Browser log forwarding
- `next-browser` (experimental)

**Turbopack:** 200+ bug fixes, SRI support, `postcss.config.ts` support, tree shaking improvements, Server Fast Refresh

### Next.js 16.2.1 (Mar 20 -- patch)

- Fix adapter outputs for dynamic metadata routes
- Turbopack: fix webpack loader runner layer
- Fix server actions in standalone mode with `cacheComponents`
- Fix layout segment optimization: move app-page imports to server-utility transition
- Turbopack: lazy require metadata and handle TLA
- Respect `{eval:true}` in `worker_threads` constructors

### Next.js 15.5.14 (Mar 19 -- backport)

- LRU disk cache for `next/image` (`images.maximumDiskCacheSize` config)
- Content-Length and ETag fix for Pages Router `/_next/data/` JSON responses

### Nx impact (HIGH)

- **Must update generators** for `proxy.ts` instead of `middleware.ts`
- Turbopack as default -- webpack config in executors should use `--webpack` for legacy
- `cacheComponents` replaces PPR experimental flags
- `next lint` removal -- Nx lint target should invoke ESLint directly
- Async request APIs enforcement -- migration generator opportunity
- Node 18 drop aligns with Nx requirements
- **16.2 Adapters API** -- evaluate if `@nx/next` should leverage adapters for custom build output
- **16.2 performance gains** are automatic; no plugin changes needed
- **`transitionTypes`** on `<Link>` is opt-in; no generator changes required
- **`postcss.config.ts`** support in Turbopack -- existing TS PostCSS configs now work

---

## React Router (formerly Remix)

**Latest stable:** react-router@7.13.1 (Feb 23)
**Legacy:** 6.30.3 (Jan 7)
**Remix 3.0:** 3.0.0-alpha.4 (Mar 25)

### Releases in window

| Version | Date   | Key changes                                                                      |
| ------- | ------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 7.13.1  | Feb 23 | Bug fixes: crossOrigin prop, double slash normalization, nonce fixes             |
| 7.13.0  | Jan 23 | **3 security fixes**: CSRF, XSS via open redirects, SSR XSS in ScrollRestoration |
| 7.12.0  | Jan 7  | Security patches                                                                 |
| 6.30.3  | Jan 7  | Legacy maintenance                                                               |

### Remix 3.0.0-alpha.4 (Mar 25) -- UPDATED

Remix 3.0 is a **complete modular rewrite**, separate from React Router:

- **Root export removed** from `remix` package -- all imports must use sub-paths (`remix/fetch-router`, `remix/component`, etc.)
- New packages: `data-schema` (schema validation), `data-table` (MySQL/Postgres/SQLite), `file-storage-s3`, `session-storage-redis`, `session-storage-memcache`
- Modular architecture: pick only what you need via sub-path exports
- Still very early alpha -- not usable for production

**New in alpha.4 (Mar 25):**

- **Security middleware**: `remix/cop-middleware` (cross-origin protection via `Sec-Fetch-Site`), `remix/csrf-middleware` (session-backed CSRF tokens), `remix/cors-middleware`
- **Auth middleware**: `remix/auth-middleware` and `remix/auth` re-exports
- **Frame navigation APIs**: `navigate()`, `link()`, `run()`, frame helpers in `remix/component`
- **Breaking**: `data-table` API restructured -- `QueryBuilder` removed, replaced by `Query`/`db.exec()`. `remix/data-table/sql` export removed (import from `remix/data-table` directly). `Database` is now a class, not just a factory return type.
- Bumped 20+ `@remix-run/*` sub-packages

### React Router v8 plans (upcoming)

- Stabilizing future flags: `v8_splitRouteModules`, `v8_viteEnvironmentApi`
- Planning to drop Node 20 support (EOL April 2026)
- Deprecating `meta` and `links` exports (removal in v9)

### Nx impact

- No breaking changes in 7.12-7.13 for `@nx/remix`.
- CSRF protection in 7.13.0 may affect apps with cross-origin form submissions.
- **Remix 3.0 alpha is a complete architecture shift** -- modular sub-path imports, no more single `remix` import. Alpha.4 adds security middleware (CSRF, CORS, COP) and auth, showing rapid feature velocity. `@nx/remix` will need significant rework if/when 3.0 stabilizes. Monitor only for now.
- v8 future flags are opt-in; plan migration support when v8 lands.

---

## Nuxt

**Latest stable:** 4.4.2 (Mar 12) | **Legacy:** 3.21.1 (Feb 7)

### Releases in window

| Version | Date   | Type         |
| ------- | ------ | ------------ |
| 4.4.2   | Mar 12 | Stable       |
| 4.3.1   | Feb 7  | Stable patch |
| 4.3.0   | Jan 22 | Stable       |
| 3.21.1  | Feb 7  | Legacy patch |
| 3.21.0  | Jan 22 | Legacy       |

### Key changes in 4.4

- **Custom `useFetch`/`useAsyncData` factories** -- create instances with default options
- **vue-router v5 upgrade** -- removes dependency on `unplugin-vue-router`, internal improvements
- **Accessibility announcer** -- `useAnnouncer` composable and `NuxtAnnouncer` component for dynamic in-page changes
- **Typed layout props**
- **Build profiling**
- **Payload extraction improvements** -- new `payloadExtraction: 'client'` mode with runtime LRU cache
- **Cookie refresh option** for session management

### Key changes in 4.3

- Route rule layouts
- ISR payload extraction
- `inlineStyles` support for webpack and rspack builders
- Hook filters for performance optimization
- Web API naming migration: `statusCode`/`statusMessage` -> `status`/`statusText` (prep for Nitro v3/H3 v2)
- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` enabled in Nitro server TS config

### Nx impact

- **vue-router v5** in Nuxt 4.4 is a notable dependency change; verify `@nx/nuxt` compatibility.
- `inlineStyles` with rspack builder (4.3) is positive for `@nx/rspack` + Nuxt users.
- Nuxt 5 preparations worth monitoring (`future.compatibilityVersion: 5` opt-in available).

## Vue

**Latest stable:** 3.5.31 (Mar 25) | **Beta:** 3.6.0-beta.9 (Mar 26)

### Releases in window

| Version         | Date    | Type           |
| --------------- | ------- | -------------- |
| 3.6.0-beta.4-9  | Jan-Mar | Beta           |
| 3.5.27-31       | Jan-Mar | Stable patches |

### Vue 3.6 beta highlights

- **Vapor Mode feature-complete** -- parity with all stable VDOM features (excluding Suspense)
- Opt-in per SFC via `<script setup vapor>` attribute
- Performance on par with Solid and Svelte 5 in benchmarks
- Recommended for partial adoption: perf-sensitive sub-pages or small new apps
- Reactivity refactor based on alien-signals -- significant perf and memory improvements

### Nx impact

- Vue 3.6 still beta; no immediate action.
- Vapor Mode is compiler-level, transparent to `@nx/vue`.
- When 3.6 goes stable, consider adding Vapor Mode option to component generators.

## Vite

**Latest stable:** 8.0.3 (Mar 26) | **Last v7:** 7.3.1 (Jan 7)

### Releases in window

| Version           | Date    | Type               |
| ----------------- | ------- | ------------------ |
| **8.0.3**         | Mar 26  | **Stable patch**   |
| **8.0.2**         | Mar 23  | **Stable patch**   |
| **8.0.1**         | Mar 19  | **Stable patch**   |
| **8.0.0**         | Mar 12  | **STABLE**         |
| 8.0.0-beta.17-18  | Mar 9   | Last betas         |
| 8.0.0-beta.6-16   | Jan-Feb | Beta (11 releases) |
| create-vite@9.0.3 | Mar 19  | Stable             |
| 7.3.1             | Jan 7   | Last v7 patch      |

### Vite 8.0.0 stable -- KEY EVENT

**Rolldown is now the single bundler**, replacing both esbuild (dep optimization) and Rollup (production builds).

**Breaking changes:**

- **`build.rollupOptions` -> `build.rolldownOptions`** (rollupOptions deprecated but compat layer auto-converts)
- **`worker.rollupOptions` -> `worker.rolldownOptions`**
- **`optimizeDeps.esbuildOptions` deprecated** -> `optimizeDeps.rolldownOptions` (compat layer auto-converts)
- **Lightning CSS** is now a normal dependency (default for CSS minification)
- **`import.meta.hot.accept` no longer accepts URLs** -- must pass id
- Package resolution: when both `browser` and `module` fields exist, uses `resolve.mainFields` order
- Extglobs not yet supported; TypeScript legacy namespace only partially supported
- **Install size ~15 MB larger** (~10 MB lightningcss, ~5 MB rolldown)

**Migration reality:** A compatibility layer auto-converts existing `esbuildOptions` and `rollupOptions` to their Rolldown/Oxc equivalents. Many projects will work without config changes. However, migration is recommended.

**Migration paths:** Direct upgrade OR gradual via `rolldown-vite` package.

### Vite 8.0.3 (Mar 26) -- patch

- Upgraded Rolldown to RC.12
- Fixed CSS style caching bug -- missing styles across multiple HTML entries
- Fixed non-ASCII character handling in base64 sourcemaps (module-runner)
- Fixed SSR `meta` identifier incorrectly rewritten inside `import.meta` when a local `meta` binding exists
- Fixed sub-path import resolution in glob imports during dep optimization
- `plugin-legacy@8.0.1` also released alongside

### Vite 8.0.2 (Mar 23) -- patch

- Upgraded Rolldown to RC.11
- Dependency updates (@vitejs/devtools ^0.1.5, other non-major deps)
- Maintenance release; no new features or breaking changes

### Vite 8.0.1 (Mar 19) -- patch

- Upgraded Rolldown to RC.10
- Fixed Lightning CSS minification with `build.target: 'es6'`
- Fixed `+` symbol handling in package subpath exports during dep optimization
- Worker output consistency fixes (require(json), client/SSR alignment)
- Properly disabled `inlineConst` optimization in bundled dev mode
- Consistent ESM Oxc runtime usage in dev
- Concurrent server restart handling improvements

### Nx impact (URGENT -- now stable with third patch)

- **`@nx/vite` must support Vite 8 as primary** -- 8.0.3 is already out
- Compat layer means existing configs won't break immediately, but generated configs should use `rolldownOptions`
- Any Nx code referencing `esbuildOptions` for dep optimization needs updating
- Lightning CSS default may affect projects with custom CSS processing
- `rolldown-vite` package useful for Nx migration generators
- **v7.3.1 is the last v7** -- plan deprecation timeline
- Test all Vite-dependent plugins: `@nx/vite`, `@nx/remix`, `@nx/vue`, `@nx/nuxt`

---

## Rolldown

**Latest:** 1.0.0-rc.12 (Mar 25)

### Releases in window

- beta.59 (Jan 7) -> beta.60 (Jan 14) -> RC.1 (Jan 22) -> weekly RCs -> RC.12 (Mar 25)

### Key developments

- **API frozen at RC** -- no breaking changes before 1.0
- 3,400+ commits since beta: 749 features, 682 fixes, 109 perf optimizations
- `output.codeSplitting` and `output.strictExecutionOrder` promoted to stable
- `tsconfig` auto-discovery enabled by default
- Built-in TS/JSX transforms via Oxc, native CJS/ESM interop
- **RC.6 dropped built-in CSS bundling** (exploring alternatives)
- 10-30x faster than Rollup, Rollup-compatible plugin API
- **Powers Vite 8.0.0+ stable**

### RC.10 highlights (Mar 18)

- Exposes `oxcRuntimePlugin` for direct use
- Fixes tree-shaking regression with `comments.annotation: false`
- Namespace member expression rewrite with optional chaining preserved
- `MagicString` `indentExclusionRanges` property
- UTF-16 index handling fixes in native MagicString

### RC.12 highlights (Mar 25)

- Skip circular dependency check when strict execution order is enabled (performance)
- Fixed non-deterministic chunk generation
- Fixed lazy-barrel loading when barrel has local exports alongside import-then-export specifiers
- Fixed correct execution order of transferred CJS init calls
- Fixed `entriesAware` calculating sizes without duplication
- Fixed `is_top_level` incorrectly treating strict-mode scopes as top-level
- Emit build warnings during watch mode rebuilds
- Treeshake: migrated SideEffectDetector to Oxc's MayHaveSideEffects trait

### RC.11 highlights (Mar 23)

- MagicString `replace` with regex support
- `output.sourcemapExcludeSources` option
- `getIndentString` and `ignoreList` support in MagicString
- Fixed `eval?.()` not reported as direct eval
- Fixed JSON float parsing with arbitrary precision
- Fixed multi-byte UTF-8 chars in hash placeholder iterator
- Removed redundant bare side-effect imports in entry/facade chunks
- Parallel AST cloning enabled on macOS (performance)
- Set `order` for callable plugins

### Nx impact

- Consumed through Vite, not directly by Nx plugins.
- CSS bundling drop in RC.6 notable -- Vite 8 handles CSS via Lightning CSS.
- Rollup plugin API compatibility means most Nx Vite plugins should work.
- 1.0 stable likely imminent given Vite 8.0.3 already ships RC.12.

---

## Rspack

**Latest stable:** 1.7.10 (Mar 24) | **Beta:** 2.0.0-beta.9 (Mar 27)

### Releases in window

| Version         | Date    | Type               |
| --------------- | ------- | ------------------ |
| 2.0.0-beta.0-9  | Feb-Mar | Beta (10 releases) |
| 2.0.0-alpha.0-1 | Jan     | Alpha              |
| 1.7.1-1.7.10    | Jan-Mar | Stable maintenance |

### Rspack 2.0 breaking changes (HIGH IMPACT)

**Infrastructure:**

- **Node 18 dropped** -- Node 20+ required
- **Pure ESM packages** -- `@rspack/core` and `@rspack/cli` ESM-only

**Config renames/removals:**

- `output.hotUpdateGlobal`: `webpackHotUpdate*` -> `rspackHotUpdate*`
- `output.chunkLoadingGlobal`: `webpackChunk*` -> `rspackChunk*`
- `experiments.rspackFuture` removed; `bundlerInfo` moves to `output`
- `experiments.lazyCompilation`, `experiments.parallelLoader`, `experiments.outputModule` removed
- `.swcrc` reading disabled by default
- `output.charset` removed
- Legacy `output.library*` options removed
- `WarnCaseSensitiveModulesPlugin` removed
- `plugin.getHooks()` removed

**Default changes:**

- `builtin:swc-loader` enables `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` by default
- `module.parser.javascript.exportsPresence` defaults to `'error'`
- `asyncWebAssembly` enabled by default
- CSS enabled by default
- `target` cascades to SWC and LightningCSS defaults (configure once)

**New in beta.5+:**

- RSC compatible with lazy compilation
- CJS require destructuring tree shaking
- `import.defer()` support
- `cache.portable` configuration

**New in beta.9 (Mar 27):**

- **`create-rspack` updated to TypeScript 6** -- confirms TS 6 adoption in Rspack ecosystem
- **`detectSyntax` option for `builtin:swc-loader`** -- auto-infers parser (TS/JSX) from file extension, reducing config boilerplate
- RSC tree-shaking for client barrels
- `import.meta.resolve()` support with tests
- ESM library fixes: star reexport namespaces, duplicate `__webpack_require__` export, external binding deconflicting
- `Symbol.for()` for shared symbol identity in module bindings

### Nx impact (HIGH)

- **ESM-only is the biggest change** -- all `require()` calls to rspack packages must become dynamic `import()` or the plugin must be ESM
- Global renames (`webpack*` -> `rspack*`) affect HMR/chunk loading and Module Federation
- `.swcrc` disabled by default -- configs relying on it need explicit opt-in
- `verbatimModuleSyntax` default may cause TS import issues
- Node 18 drop aligned with ecosystem direction
- **Rspack 2.0 adopting TS 6** in `create-rspack` (beta.9) confirms ecosystem momentum -- Nx must keep pace
- `detectSyntax` option could simplify `@nx/rspack` generated configs (less explicit parser config needed)
- Beta.9 is latest -- stable approaching, likely Q2 2026

---

## esbuild

**Latest:** 0.27.4 (Mar 12)

Two patch releases in window (0.27.3, 0.27.4). Development has slowed as Rolldown/Vite 8 transition is now live. No breaking changes.

### Nx impact

- `@nx/esbuild` stable. With Vite 8 shipping Rolldown, esbuild's role is diminishing in the Vite ecosystem.
- Long-term, monitor Rolldown as potential replacement for `@nx/esbuild`.

---

## SWC

**Latest:** @swc/core 1.15.21 (Mar 22)

Active release cadence with ~10 releases in window (1.15.10 through 1.15.21). Bug fixes and performance improvements. Wasm plugin backward compatibility since 1.15.0. No major API changes.

### Nx impact

- No breaking changes for `@nx/js` SWC compilation.
- Rspack 2.0's `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` default for builtin:swc-loader worth noting.

---

## Cross-Cutting: TypeScript 6.0 STABLE -- CRITICAL EVENT

**Beta:** February 11, 2026 | **Stable (6.0.2):** March 24, 2026
**Announcement:** https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-6-0/

TypeScript 6.0 is now GA. This is the **most impactful single release for Nx** in this cycle because Nx's default tsconfig base and every plugin's generator emit tsconfig options that are now deprecated or have changed defaults.

### Breaking changes and new defaults

| Change                               | Impact                                                          | Nx tsconfig base affected? |
| ------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| `strict` defaults to `true`          | Existing projects without explicit `strict` will get strictness | **YES** -- base omits it   |
| `module` defaults to `esnext`        | Generator tsconfig updates needed                               | YES                        |
| `target` defaults to `es2025`        | Safe for modern projects                                        | YES                        |
| `types` defaults to `[]`             | Projects relying on ambient `@types/*` will break               | **YES** -- base omits it   |
| `rootDir` default changed            | May affect project references and composite builds              | **YES**                    |
| `moduleResolution: node` deprecated  | Must migrate to `bundler`/`nodenext`                            | **YES** -- base uses it    |
| `baseUrl` deprecated                 | Use `paths` with `#/` imports instead                           | **YES** -- base uses it    |
| `target: es5` deprecated             | Breaks legacy configurations                                    | Affects old projects       |
| `--outFile` removed                  | Breaks bundled output projects                                  | Rare                       |
| `--moduleResolution classic` removed | Must migrate to `node`/`bundler`/`nodenext`                     | No (Nx uses `node`)        |
| `esModuleInterop: false` forbidden   | Must be true or omitted                                         | YES                        |

### Specific Nx tsconfig base impacts

Nx's `tsconfig.base.json` template typically includes:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": ".",           // DEPRECATED in TS 6
    "moduleResolution": "node", // DEPRECATED in TS 6 (use "bundler" or "nodenext")
    // "types" omitted        // NOW defaults to [] instead of all @types/*
    // "strict" omitted       // NOW defaults to true
  }
}
```

**This means:** Every new `npm install @types/node` or `@types/jest` will NOT be auto-included unless `types` is explicitly configured. Projects that relied on ambient type acquisition will break silently.

### Nx action required -- IMMEDIATE

1. **Audit `tsconfig.base.json` generator** -- must explicitly set `types` array (at minimum `["node"]`) and migrate `moduleResolution` from `"node"` to `"bundler"`
2. **Audit `baseUrl` usage** -- Nx uses `baseUrl: "."` for path mapping; must migrate to explicit `paths` entries with `#/` prefix or keep `baseUrl` with suppressed deprecation warning
3. **Create TS 6 migration generator** -- update existing workspaces:
   - Add explicit `types` array to `tsconfig.base.json`
   - Migrate `moduleResolution: "node"` to `"bundler"` (for app projects) or `"nodenext"` (for publishable libs)
   - Replace `baseUrl` with explicit `paths` or document the deprecation
   - Ensure `esModuleInterop` is `true` or omitted (not `false`)
4. **Test all framework plugins with TS 6.0.2** -- Angular 21.2 already claims TS 6 compat; verify Next.js, Vue, Nuxt, Vite
5. **Update `create-nx-workspace` templates** to emit TS 6-compatible configs
6. **Document the migration path** -- this will affect every Nx workspace upgrading to TS 6

---

## Cross-Cutting: Remix 3.0 Modular Rewrite

Remix 3.0 alpha represents a fundamental architectural shift from framework to modular toolkit:

- No more single `remix` import -- everything via sub-paths
- Built-in data layer: `data-schema` (validation), `data-table` (SQL backends)
- Built-in storage: `file-storage-s3`, `session-storage-redis`, `session-storage-memcache`
- **New in alpha.4**: Built-in security middleware (CSRF, CORS, COP), auth middleware, frame navigation
- Separate from React Router (which continues as v7/v8)

This is early (alpha.4) but the pace of feature additions (alpha.3 -> alpha.4 in 25 days with 20+ package bumps) suggests active development. Will require `@nx/remix` rethinking when it stabilizes.

---
